payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields should capture what model was used, how many tokens were consumed, estimated cost, and observed latency distribution. Token counts usually include input/prompt tokens, output/completion tokens, total tokens, and sometimes cached or reasoning tokens if the provider reports them. Provider/model IDs identify the serving provider and exact model or deployment used, for example provider_id and model_id, so cost and latency can be attributed correctly. Estimated price is the calculated cost for the request or eval run, typically derived from token counts multiplied by provider/model pricing rates, and should include currency, pricing version or timestamp if available, and any assumptions. Latency p50, p90, and p95 are percentile latency metrics across requests: p50 is the median latency, p90 means 90% of requests completed at or below that duration, and p95 means 95% completed at or below that duration. These should specify units, usually milliseconds, and define whether latency is end-to-end, provider-only, time-to-first-token, or time-to-last-token.

entity_resolution.reason: The input is a request for an explanation of evaluation accounting fields, not a durable user memory fact.

payload.intent: Explain required eval accounting fields: token counts, provider/model IDs, estimated price, and latency percentiles p50/p90/p95.

details: No memory was committed because the input contained no durable personal fact.
Provided concise debug explanation of the requested accounting fields.
